findings.message: Breaking schema change: field renamed from 'name' to 'title' without backwards-compatible migration path. This breaks existing GraphQL clients querying the 'name' field.
findings.suggestion: Either maintain 'name' as the field name or introduce a deprecation directive (@deprecated) on 'name' and keep both fields during a transition period. Coordinate this change with REST API (ItemRead still uses 'name' in src/demo/routes/items.py).

findings.message: GraphQL/REST API divergence: the Item entity is exposed as 'title' in GraphQL but 'name' in REST (ItemRead in routes/items.py line 22). Different field names for the same concept across APIs create inconsistency and confuse client developers.
findings.suggestion: Standardize the field name across both APIs. Either rename REST ItemRead.name to 'title', or rename GraphQL Item.title back to 'name' to match the database model and REST API.

findings.message: Unbounded list field: the 'items' query returns list[Item] with no pagination. This can cause performance issues and excessive data transfer if the items collection grows large.
findings.suggestion: Add pagination arguments (e.g., 'first' and 'after' for cursor-based pagination, or 'limit' and 'offset') to the items field. Use Strawberry's connection types or a custom pagination wrapper to ensure scalability.
